evals.prompt: I'm debugging a Mina node and have ~/.mina-config/internal-tracing/internal-trace.jsonl plus prover-internal-trace.jsonl. Figure out what kind of trace these are, summarize the biggest gaps, and tell me where the checkpoints come from in the repo.
evals.expected_output: Classifies these as OCaml Mina internal tracing JSONL files, uses the bundled summarizer or equivalent parsing, includes prover subprocess file, and points to src/lib/internal_tracing plus [%log internal] producers.

evals.prompt: Does mina client start-internal-tracing turn on the internal_tracing::checkpoint! calls in proof-systems/kimchi/src/prover.rs? Investigate the repo before answering.
evals.expected_output: Explains the distinction between OCaml Internal_tracing and Rust proof-system internal-tracing, cites the CLI/RPC/toggle path and the absence of production start_tracing/take_traces usage for Kimchi traces.

evals.prompt: I have trace/12345.trace from running mina daemon --tracing. Convert or inspect it and don't confuse it with internal-trace.jsonl.
evals.expected_output: Classifies this as O1Trace/WebKit binary tracing, points to Mina_tracing and src/app/trace-tool, and does not use the JSONL internal trace workflow.
